Clinical Reasoning:
The key information points out that dental plaque contains a protective matrix primarily made of extracellular polysaccharides and glycoproteins, making it very resistant to antimicrobial action. Therefore, the correct reason why dental plaque adheres to the tooth lies in the composition of its adhesive components rather than simply physical properties.
\boxed{A}
My analysis focuses on understanding how the structural components of plaque allow it to adhere effectively despite attempts to dislodge it, ruling out incorrect options related solely to physical stickiness or intrinsic properties of certain sugars or enzymes. Clinical Reasoning:
The key information points out that dental plaque contains a protective matrix primarily made of extracellular polysaccharides and glycoproteins, making it very resistant to antimicrobial action. Therefore, the correct reason why dental plaque adheres to the tooth lies in the composition of its adhesive components rather than simply physical properties.
\boxed{A}
My analysis focuses on understanding how the structural components of plaque allow it to adhere effectively despite attempts to dislodge it, ruling out incorrect options related solely to physical stickiness or intrinsic properties of certain sugars or enzymes. Option C might seem plausible given biological processes involved, yet the evidence emphasizes structural integrity more directly tied to adhesion mechanisms. Hence, I chose option A emphasizing levans' ability to create a gummy substance aiding adherence. \boxed{A} Based on the detailed evidence provided, particularly focusing on the description of the plaque biofilm matrix containing extracellular polysaccharides and glycoproteins which confer resistance to antimicrobials and facilitate adhesion, we can deduce that the adhesive quality of dental plaque stems fundamentally from biochemical composition rather than simple physical traits mentioned in some other options. Thus,
\boxed{A} accurately reflects the core explanation derived from the physiological aspects outlined in the text regarding plaque formation and its tenacious hold onto tooth surfaces.
